topic: Create and configure Azure storage accounts
key_facts:
Section

 Field

 Required or optional

 Description

 
 
 
 Security

 Require secure transfer for REST API operations

 Optional

 Require secure transfer to ensure that incoming requests to this storage account are made only via HTTPS (default).

Enable support for customer-managed keys

 Required

 By default, customer managed keys can be used to encrypt only blobs and files.

Section

 Field

 Required or optional

 Description

 
 
 
 Network connectivity

 Network access

 Required

 By default, incoming network traffic is routed to the public endpoint for your storage account.

Network connectivity

 Endpoint type

 Required

 Azure Storage supports two types of endpoints: standard endpoints (the default) and Azure DNS zone endpoints (preview).
1 With a quota increase, you can create up to 500 storage accounts with standard endpoints per region in a given subscription, for a total of 5500 storage accounts per region.
Storage account names must be between 3 and 24 characters in length and might contain numbers and lowercase letters only.

Security

 Enable storage account key access

 Optional

 When enabled, this setting allows clients to authorize requests to the storage account using either the account access keys or a Microsoft Entra account (default).
